Row1.Rationale: Nivolumab binds PD‑1 with high affinity, blocking interaction with PD‑L1/PD‑L2 and restoring antitumor T‑cell proliferation and effector function; PD‑L1 expression on NSCLC tumors and tumor‑infiltrating immune cells correlates with higher ORR and OS to PD‑1/PD‑L1 inhibitors.

Row2.Rationale: Smoking‑related NSCLC has high TMB, increasing neoantigen load and immunogenicity; high TMB independently predicts improved PFS and response to nivolumab±ipilimumab compared with chemotherapy in advanced NSCLC and is being used to refine patient selection beyond PD‑L1 alone.

Row3.Rationale: STK11/LKB1 loss defines a “KL” phenotype with low PD‑L1, reduced CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration, and immune‑desert microenvironment; in KRAS‑mutant LUAD, STK11 alterations are the dominant genomic driver of primary resistance to PD‑1 blockade with markedly reduced ORR, PFS, and OS to nivolumab and other ICIs.

Row4.Rationale: KEAP1 mutations constitutively activate NRF2, driving oxidative‑stress adaptation, metabolic reprogramming, and immune exclusion; KEAP1‑mutant NSCLC shows reduced T‑cell recruitment, downregulation of STING/TLR inflammatory genes, and inferior benefit from PD‑(L)1 monotherapy, particularly in KRAS‑mutant adenocarcinoma.

Row5.Rationale: KRAS mutations associate with smoking, high TMB, PD‑L1 expression and CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration, and meta‑analysis shows KRAS‑mutant NSCLC has better OS and PFS on ICI monotherapy than KRAS‑WT; however, KRAS/STK11 or KRAS/KEAP1 co‑mutations create an immune‑cold, myeloid‑rich TME with poor nivolumab outcomes.

Row6.Rationale: Type I/II IFN signaling via JAK/STAT upregulates HLA and PD‑L1 and supports T‑cell recruitment; NSCLC with JAK1/2 mutations or transcriptional downregulation of IFN‑response genes at progression shows impaired PD‑L1 induction, reduced antigen presentation, and is implicated in primary and acquired resistance to PD‑1 blockade.

Row7.Rationale: Loss or downregulation of HLA class I/β2‑microglobulin abrogates CD8⁺ T‑cell recognition; NSCLC with impaired MHC processing or HLA loss is enriched among non‑responders and in tumors with acquired resistance to nivolumab, and epigenetic repression of these genes is recognized as a key mechanism of ICI failure.

Row8.Rationale: In NSCLC, high adenosine signaling suppresses effector T‑cell and NK‑cell function and expands Tregs; elevated CD39/CD73 and adenosine‑generating ectoenzymes, together with high kynurenine/tryptophan ratios, are linked to early progression on nivolumab and other PD‑1/PD‑L1 antibodies.

Row9.Rationale: VEGF signaling promotes aberrant angiogenesis, impairs dendritic‑cell maturation, recruits Tregs and myeloid‑derived suppressor cells, and restricts cytotoxic T‑cell infiltration; VEGF‑driven immune‑excluded TMEs are a major barrier to PD‑1 efficacy, and anti‑VEGF combinations aim to normalize vasculature and re‑sensitize NSCLC to ICIs, though phase III data remain mixed.

Row10.Rationale: IFN‑γ–induced IDO depletes tryptophan and accumulates immunosuppressive kynurenine, inhibiting effector T‑cell activation and promoting Treg differentiation; NSCLC patients with early progression on nivolumab exhibit significantly higher kynurenine/tryptophan ratios, implicating IDO‑mediated metabolic suppression in primary PD‑1 resistance.

Row11.Rationale: Lung cancer TMEs often contain abundant M2‑like TAMs and MDSCs that secrete IL‑10/TGF‑β, exclude CD8⁺ T cells, and sustain PD‑1 resistance; however, in a PD‑L1‑negative EGFR‑mutant adenocarcinoma, dense PD‑L1⁺ TAMs with CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration coincided with a >2‑year durable nivolumab response, suggesting that PD‑L1⁺ TAM‑rich niches can also serve as targets for PD‑1 blockade.

Row12.Rationale: Concomitant PPI use reshapes gut microbiota, enriching regulatory T‑cell–associated species and systemic inflammation; metastatic NSCLC patients receiving nivolumab with PPIs have significantly shorter PFS, and broader ICI cohorts show that PPI exposure within ±30 days of ICI start and high cumulative PPI dose over the prior year are independently associated with reduced OS.

Row13.Rationale: Nivolumab binds PD‑1 and prevents PD‑L1/PD‑L2 engagement, reversing SHP‑2–mediated inhibition of TCR signaling and restoring PI3K–AKT/MAPK activation, cytokine production, and cytotoxicity; higher PD‑L1 expression on NSCLC tumor and immune cells is broadly associated with improved response rates and survival to PD‑1/PD‑L1 inhibitors.

Row14.Rationale: Smoking‑associated NSCLC often has high TMB, increasing neoantigen load and immunogenicity; across ICI‑treated NSCLC cohorts, high TMB correlates with better PFS/OS and higher response to nivolumab±ipilimumab compared with chemotherapy, and is used to refine selection beyond PD‑L1 alone.

Row15.Rationale: STK11/LKB1 loss in KRAS‑mutant LUAD drives an “immune‑cold” phenotype with low PD‑L1, sparse CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration, and myeloid‑dominant TME; this KL genotype shows markedly reduced ORR, PFS, and OS to PD‑1 blockade, identifying STK11 as a dominant genomic driver of primary resistance.

Row16.Rationale: KEAP1 mutations constitutively activate NRF2, promoting oxidative‑stress tolerance, metabolic rewiring, and suppression of STING/TLR inflammatory signaling; KEAP1‑mutant NSCLC exhibits immune exclusion and inferior benefit from PD‑(L)1 monotherapy, especially in KRAS‑mutant adenocarcinoma.

Row17.Rationale: Oncogenic KRAS in NSCLC associates with smoking, high TMB, PD‑L1 expression, and CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration; meta‑analyses show improved OS/PFS on ICI monotherapy vs KRAS‑WT. However, KRAS/STK11 or KRAS/KEAP1 co‑mutations generate myeloid‑rich, non‑inflamed TMEs with poor nivolumab outcomes.

Row18.Rationale: IFN‑γ produced by reinvigorated T cells activates JAK/STAT to upregulate HLA and PD‑L1 and drive chemokines that recruit effector cells; NSCLC with JAK1/2 mutations or transcriptional suppression of IFN‑response genes shows impaired antigen presentation and is implicated in primary and acquired resistance to PD‑1 blockade.

Row19.Pathway ID/Name: Antigen processing and presentation (HLA‑I / β2‑microglobulin; KEGG hsa04612)
Row19.Rationale: Effective nivolumab responses require intact MHC‑I presentation; NSCLC with loss‑of‑function alterations or epigenetic repression of HLA‑I/β2‑microglobulin display reduced CD8⁺ T‑cell recognition and are enriched among non‑responders and tumors with acquired resistance to PD‑1/PD‑L1 blockade.

Row20.Pathway ID/Name: PI3K–AKT pathway and tumor‑intrinsic PD‑L1 regulation (KEGG hsa04151)
Row20.Rationale: PTEN loss and PI3K–AKT activation in NSCLC upregulate PD‑L1 and foster an immunosuppressive TME with reduced T‑cell infiltration; this oncogenic signaling is linked to primary resistance to ICIs, while modulation of PTEN/AMPK and related metabolic checkpoints can influence sensitivity to PD‑1 blockade.

Row21.Rationale: VEGF‑driven aberrant vasculature limits T‑cell trafficking, impairs dendritic‑cell maturation, and recruits Tregs and MDSCs; NSCLC with high angiogenic signatures shows “cold” immune profiles and reduced benefit from PD‑1 monotherapy, providing a rationale for anti‑VEGF plus nivolumab combinations.

Row22.Rationale: In lung tumors, ectonucleotidases CD39/CD73 convert ATP to adenosine, which signals via A2A receptors to suppress effector T and NK cells and expand Tregs; elevated adenosine‑pathway activity and high kynurenine/tryptophan ratios are associated with early progression on nivolumab and other PD‑1/PD‑L1 antibodies.

Row23.Pathway ID/Name: Alternative immune checkpoints and exhaustion program (e.g., LAG‑3, TIM‑3, VISTA)
Row23.Rationale: Under sustained PD‑1 inhibition, exhausted T cells upregulate non‑PD‑1 inhibitory receptors such as LAG‑3, TIM‑3 and VISTA; this compensatory exhaustion circuitry maintains T‑cell dysfunction and mediates secondary resistance to nivolumab, motivating dual‑checkpoint strategies.

Row24.Pathway ID/Name: Gut microbiome–immune modulation and PPI‑associated dysbiosis
Row24.Rationale: Concomitant proton‑pump inhibitor use reshapes gut microbiota, diminishing diversity and favoring Treg‑associated species; in metastatic NSCLC and broader ICI cohorts, PPI exposure around nivolumab/ICI initiation and high cumulative PPI dose over the prior year independently predict shorter PFS and OS, implicating microbiome‑mediated resistance.
